archetype: Atreus, Impulsive Son // Kratos, Stoic Father
overview: The deck leverages a dual-state engine to fluidly shift between explosive tempo aggression and resilient control, using Iroas, God of Victory as a combat and value anchor. It aims to out-resource and out-adapt midrange, control, and combo archetypes by dictating pace, trading efficiently, and closing out games through combat advantage or late-game inevitability.
win_conditions: Iroas, God of Victory as a combat finisher or value engine.
Kratos/Atreus in Impulsive mode with pumped stats.
Late-game card advantage engines to out-value control and close out games through incremental damage or combat tricks.

keep_criteria: Keep hands with 2-3 lands (including at least one Arcane Signet or a dual land capable of producing R/U/W), Kratos/Atreus, and a mix of turn 1-2 interaction or a finisher.
mulligan_criteria: Mulligan if you have zero lands, only single-color sources with no Signet, or a hand composed entirely of high-cost spells with no early plays or mana fixing.

early_game: Turn 1: Play a land, cast Arcane Signet if available, or deploy a cheap creature/spell to establish board presence.
Turn 2: Cast Kratos/Atreus, choosing Stoic mode to draw/filter or Impulsive mode to attack/trigger effects.
Sequence lands to ensure R/U/W availability by turn 3, prioritizing Signet drops to accelerate into key spells.
mid_game: Maintain a steady R/U/W mana base and use Arcane Signet to accelerate into pivotal spells.
Manage your hand by trading early threats for interaction, saving Iroas for late-game combat or value triggers.
keep options open to adapt to the board state.
Commit threats when you have interaction to clear blockers or answer responses.
Hold back if the opponent has sweepers or heavy removal.
Use Kratos's defensive capabilities to stabilize, then pivot to Impulsive aggression when it is safe to do so.
late_game: Iroas, God of Victory as a combat finisher or value engine.
Kratos/Atreus in Impulsive mode with pumped stats.
Late-game card advantage engines to out-value control and close out games through incremental damage or combat tricks.
Go all-in when you have removal for their answer, have established board presence, or when the opponent is at lethal range.
Commit when you can trade efficiently or when you've drawn into your win condition and they lack interaction.

sequencing_tips: Common mistake: Overcommitting to Impulsive mode without backup, leaving you vulnerable to sweepers.
Always maintain a balance between the two states.
Iroas synergizes best with combat-heavy or +1/+1 counter strategies, so track opponent's board state carefully.
Arcane Signet is crucial for tempo
don't leave it untapped if you can cast a spell.
The 'Unknown' format may have specific rules about the dual-state mechanic
verify timing, priority, and stacking order before tournament play.

matchups.advice: Use layered spells (impulsive mode first, stoic mode second) and cast around counters with cheap threats. Use Arcane Signet to cast multiple spells in a turn, creating resolution windows. Employ uncounterable elements or protection if available.
Apply early pressure to disrupt their mana development. Use removal on key ramp creatures and hold up interaction for their big threats. Don't overextend into their late-game finishers.

Stabilize with early removal and defensive Kratos plays. Don't overextend; trade efficiently and survive to turn 4-5. Post-sideboard, prioritize board wipes and direct damage to handle token or swarm strategies.

Pressure with resilient threats and card advantage to out-value them. Force them to use removal on multiple targets. Pivot to a longer game if they have sweepers, and use Iroas to win combat exchanges they can't answer.

Win the resource war by prioritizing hand disruption and targeted removal. Exploit the dual-state mechanic by forcing them to guess your mode. Win combat exchanges with Iroas advantage and maintain better card selection.

common_threats: Watch for sweepers (Krenko's goblin tokens, Ur-Dragon's board presence), counterspells (Prismari), and combo pieces (Y'shtola, Night's Blessed).
Key tells include opponents holding up mana on their turn, playing cheap instants/artifacts, or sacrificing creatures for incremental value.
